title: askellia nana subsp. ramosa
intro: Askellia nana subsp. ramosa, also known as the shrubby pyrethrum, is a flowering plant species within the Asteraceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote healing, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies. Askellia nana subsp. ramosa is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or poultice for topical application, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
